title: Google best-practices docs and Cobra migration
description: Track documentation hardening to Google Go best practices, runnable examples, and ddsync migration to Cobra CLI.
acceptance_criteria: All child tasks are completed: exported APIs are properly documented, example_test coverage is added, and ddsync is migrated to Cobra with updated tests/docs/CI.

title: M3: Migrate ddsync CLI to Cobra
description: Replace manual flag dispatch with Cobra command tree and dedicated command package structure.
acceptance_criteria: ddsync uses Cobra root/subcommands, supports update/verify/status plus version/completion, and exposes optional --json output mode.

title: M2: Add runnable examples in example_test.go
description: Create core API examples in example_test.go for constructor, parse flows, options, and client hints helper.
acceptance_criteria: go test runs all Example functions successfully, and examples cover Parse, ParseWithClientHints, ParseWithHeaders, and key options.

title: M1: GoDoc pass across ddgo, cmd/ddsync, and internal/ddsync
description: Audit and improve exported symbol comments and package docs to align with Google Go best practices. Include concurrency and sentinel semantics where relevant.
acceptance_criteria: All exported identifiers in scoped packages have proper Godoc comments; package-level docs exist for ddgo, cmd/ddsync, and internal/ddsync.

title: M4: Align tests, README, and CI with new ddsync/docs flows
description: Update command tests, user docs, and CI checks to match Cobra-based CLI and documentation/example expectations.
acceptance_criteria: CLI tests pass, README commands reflect new CLI behavior, and CI validates go test, race tests, and ddsync verify with updated invocation paths.

title: OSS maintainer baseline hardening
description: Bring repository hygiene, licensing, linting, module integrity checks, and maintainer docs to strict Go OSS baseline.
acceptance_criteria: Top-level license and hygiene files are present, lint/module checks are automated in CI, and maintainer documentation is complete.

title: M1: Add top-level MIT licensing and policy notes
description: Add root LICENSE for project code and document interplay with third-party LGPL-derived data notices.
acceptance_criteria: Repository has top-level MIT LICENSE and docs clearly distinguish project license from third-party data licensing requirements.

title: M4: Add module integrity checks and maintainer runbook updates
description: Integrate go mod verify/tidy checks into CI and update README/maintainer docs with support policy and release hygiene.
acceptance_criteria: CI enforces module integrity checks and docs include maintainer workflow for modules, releases, and support expectations.

title: M2: Add repository hygiene files (.aiignore/.dockerignore)
description: Introduce standard OSS maintainer hygiene files for AI tooling and Docker build context control.
acceptance_criteria: Root .aiignore and .dockerignore exist with sane defaults and are referenced in maintainer docs.

title: M3: Add golangci configuration and lint workflow
description: Add .golangci.yml and dedicated GitHub Actions lint workflow using go-version-file from go.mod.
acceptance_criteria: Lint config exists and CI runs golangci-lint on pull requests and pushes with deterministic version pinning.

title: Port Matomo Device Detector to Go
description: Near-full parity Go port with regex sync pipeline and LGPL-compliant distribution.
acceptance_criteria: Public API, parser pipeline, sync tooling, parity tests, and release docs are complete.

title: M1: Bootstrap Go module and public API skeleton
description: Initialize module structure, detector API surface, and core result types.
acceptance_criteria: go.mod, package layout, constructor/options, and Parse API compile with baseline tests.

title: M2: Build regex sync tool (ddsync) with pinned snapshots
description: Implement sync pipeline from upstream regex repo to deterministic local artifacts plus manifest metadata.
acceptance_criteria: ddsync update/verify/status works; generated outputs are deterministic and committed.

title: M3: Implement parser engines for bot/client/os/device
description: Port matching logic and precedence to support near-full category detection.
acceptance_criteria: Core parsers return expected structured results for representative UA corpus.

title: M4: Add normalization, aliases, and client hints
description: Implement UA normalization behaviors, alias mappings, and Sec-CH-UA style hints integration.
acceptance_criteria: Alias + hints paths are covered with tests and consistent output semantics.

title: M5: Parity test harness and golden fixtures
description: Create differential and golden tests against upstream fixtures and expected outputs.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs parity suite; regressions produce readable diffs.

title: M6: Performance and concurrency hardening
description: Precompile/caching strategy, concurrency safety, and benchmarks for common UAs.
acceptance_criteria: Benchmarks and race tests pass with documented performance envelope.

title: M7: Licensing and compliance packaging
description: Bundle upstream notices, LICENSE copies, provenance manifest, and redistribution docs.
acceptance_criteria: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES and source provenance are complete and verified in CI.

title: M8: CI/release wiring and maintenance runbook
description: Finalize CI checks, sync cadence, release checklist, and update workflow docs.
acceptance_criteria: go test/race/sync verify/license checks are in CI; README includes sync + release flow.
